The king mentioned in this text cannot be identified with any of the historical monarchs of Egypt, although his personal name, Rameses, is sufficiently common among the rulers of the 20th dynasty. Human sacrifice was practiced in Egypt throughout its various periods. Amasis II of the 25th dynasty put an end to human sacrifice at Heliopolis.
